content.problemStatement:
Alice has two arrays AAA and BBB, each of length NNN.

She wants an array CCC of length 2‚ãÖN2\cdot N2‚ãÖN using both the arrays such that:


Each element of the arrays AAA and BBB is used exactly once;
The ithi^{th}ith element of array AAA takes either (2‚ãÖi)th(2\cdot i)^{th}(2‚ãÖi)th or (2‚ãÖi‚àí1)th(2\cdot i-1)^{th}(2‚ãÖi‚àí1)th position in CCC;
The ithi^{th}ith element of array BBB takes either (2‚ãÖi)th(2\cdot i)^{th}(2‚ãÖi)th or (2‚ãÖi‚àí1)th(2\cdot i-1)^{th}(2‚ãÖi‚àí1)th position in CCC.
Alice will make the first move by choosing any 1‚â§i‚â§N1 \leq i \leq N1‚â§i‚â§N and placing AiA_iAi‚Äã at a suitable position in CCC.

Bob will make rest of the moves and place all the remaining elements of arrays AAA and BBB in array CCC.

Let F(C)F(C)F(C) denote the **maximum subarray sum** in array CCC.

Alice wants to minimize F(C)F(C)F(C) while Bob wants to maximize it. If both make their moves optimally, find the final value of F(C)F(C)F(C).

Note:


An **empty subarray** has sum 000.
A subarray is obtained by deleting some (possibly zero) elements from the beginning and some (possibly zero) elements from the end of the array.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN ‚Äî the number of elements in AAA and BBB.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the array AAA.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã denoting the array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** subarray sum in array CCC, if both make their moves optimally.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 4‚ãÖ1054 \cdot 10^54‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Let Alice chose i=2i = 2i=2 and placed A2A_2A2‚Äã at C3C_3C3‚Äã. Then, Bob can form
C=[A1,B1,A2,B2,A3,B3]=[1,2,‚àí1,‚àí2,‚àí1,3]C = [A_1, B_1, A_2, B_2, A_3, B_3] = [1, 2, -1, -2, -1, 3]C=[A1‚Äã,B1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,B2‚Äã,A3‚Äã,B3‚Äã]=[1,2,‚àí1,‚àí2,‚àí1,3].
The subarray [1,2][1, 2][1,2] has sum 333. It can be shown that this is the maximum subarray sum if both make their moves optimally.
Test case 222: All possible arrays CCC are:

[A1,B1,A2,B2]=[1,4,‚àí1,5][A_1, B_1, A_2, B_2] = [1, 4, -1, 5][A1‚Äã,B1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,B2‚Äã]=[1,4,‚àí1,5]
[A1,B1,B2,A2]=[1,4,5,‚àí1][A_1, B_1, B_2, A_2] = [1, 4, 5, -1][A1‚Äã,B1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,A2‚Äã]=[1,4,5,‚àí1]
[B1,A1,A2,B2]=[4,1,‚àí1,5][B_1, A_1, A_2, B_2] = [4, 1, -1, 5][B1‚Äã,A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,B2‚Äã]=[4,1,‚àí1,5]
[B1,A1,B2,A2]=[4,1,5,‚àí1][B_1, A_1, B_2, A_2] = [4, 1, 5, -1][B1‚Äã,A1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,A2‚Äã]=[4,1,5,‚àí1]

Alice will choose i=2i = 2i=2 and will place A2A_2A2‚Äã at C3C_3C3‚Äã.
Bob can make CCC with maximum subarray sum of 1+4‚àí1+5=91+4-1+5 = 91+4‚àí1+5=9, which can be obtained using arrays 111 and 333.
Test case 333: All elements in CCC are negative. Thus, irrespective of the moves, an empty subarray will give the maximum subarray sum, which is 000.
